definitions: A pseudoscience primarily focused on the measurements of the human skull, based on the concept that the brain is the organ of the mind, and that certain brain areas have specific functions or modules.
The study of the shape and size of the cranium as a supposed indication of character and mental abilities.
examples: Phrenology was popular in the 19th century.
She studied phrenology to understand personality.
The museum exhibit featured phrenology busts.
